channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. B
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review B
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org

item.title: Pairing-induced momentum-space magnetism and its implication in optical anomalous Hall effect in chiral superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Bin Geng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

The intrinsic mechanisms of the magneto-optical Kerr signal in chiral superconductors often involve multiorbital degrees of freedom. Here, by considering a generic single-orbital and spinful Hamiltonian, we generalize the Onsager relation to obtain the necessary conditions for the optical anomalous …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094527] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bin Geng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu

The intrinsic mechanisms of the magneto-optical Kerr signal in chiral superconductors often involve multiorbital degrees of freedom. Here, by considering a generic single-orbital and spinful Hamiltonian, we generalize the Onsager relation to obtain the necessary conditions for the optical anomalous …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094527] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Pairing-induced momentum-space magnetism and its implication in optical anomalous Hall effect in chiral superconductors
item.dc:creator: Bin Geng, Yang Gao, and Qian Niu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Exceptional-point induced dynamic sensitivity to particle-number parity
item.description:
Author(s): J. Y. Liu-Sun and Z. Song

As an exclusive feature of a non-Hermitian system, the existence of exceptional points (EPs) depends not only on the details of the Hamiltonian but also on the particle-number filling and the particle statistics. In this paper, we study many-particle EPs in a Bose-Hubbard chain with two end-site res…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115158] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): J. Y. Liu-Sun and Z. Song

As an exclusive feature of a non-Hermitian system, the existence of exceptional points (EPs) depends not only on the details of the Hamiltonian but also on the particle-number filling and the particle statistics. In this paper, we study many-particle EPs in a Bose-Hubbard chain with two end-site res…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115158] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Exceptional-point induced dynamic sensitivity to particle-number parity
item.dc:creator: J. Y. Liu-Sun and Z. Song

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Multipole-mediated interactions in an altermagnetic ordered state in ${\mathrm{TbPt}}_{6}{\mathrm{Al}}_{3}$ with a rare-earth honeycomb lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Isao Ishii, Kentaro Isobe, Ryohei Oishi, Kanna Sunazaki, and Toshiro Takabatake

We report on ultrasonic measurements of the rare-earth altermagnet ${\mathrm{TbPt}}_{6}{\mathrm{Al}}_{3}$ in which the Tb magnetic moments in a honeycomb lattice form a collinear antiferromagnetic structure below ${T}_{\mathrm{N}}=3.4\phantom{\rule{0.28em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$. All elastic moduli show a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125149] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Isao Ishii, Kentaro Isobe, Ryohei Oishi, Kanna Sunazaki, and Toshiro Takabatake

We report on ultrasonic measurements of the rare-earth altermagnet TbPt6Al3 in which the Tb magnetic moments in a honeycomb lattice form a collinear antiferromagnetic structure below TN=3.4K. All elastic moduli show a distinct downward peak at TQ=1.6K, suggesting an additional phase transition. The …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125149] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Multipole-mediated interactions in an altermagnetic ordered state in ${\mathrm{TbPt}}_{6}{\mathrm{Al}}_{3}$ with a rare-earth honeycomb lattice
item.dc:creator: Isao Ishii, Kentaro Isobe, Ryohei Oishi, Kanna Sunazaki, and Toshiro Takabatake
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Microscopic theory of the inverse spin galvanic effect in anisotropic Rashba models
item.description:
Author(s): Alessandro Veneri, Francesco Quintavalle, Thierry Valet, and Roberto Raimondi

The Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) is a well-known mechanism for the spin-charge interconversion via the inverse and direct spin galvanic effects. The lack of a full inversion symmetry allows the coupling of the charge current and spin density. In this paper we investigate this phenomenon when the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125432] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Alessandro Veneri, Francesco Quintavalle, Thierry Valet, and Roberto Raimondi

The Rashba spin-orbit coupling (SOC) is a well-known mechanism for the spin-charge interconversion via the inverse and direct spin galvanic effects. The lack of a full inversion symmetry allows the coupling of the charge current and spin density. In this paper we investigate this phenomenon when the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125432] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic theory of the inverse spin galvanic effect in anisotropic Rashba models
item.dc:creator: Alessandro Veneri, Francesco Quintavalle, Thierry Valet, and Roberto Raimondi

item.title: Superconductivity in the kagomelike ${\mathrm{MgP}}_{2}{\mathrm{H}}_{3}$ under high pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Jianning Zhang, Xiuxing Zhang, Bing He, and Lianqiang Xu

The pursuit of superconductors combining kagomelike features represents a cutting-edge research challenge. Through crystal structure prediction and first-principles calculations, we systematically explore the Mg-P-H ternary system at 200 GPa, identifying two stable phases: kagome-structured ${\mathr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094525] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jianning Zhang, Xiuxing Zhang, Bing He, and Lianqiang Xu

The pursuit of superconductors combining kagomelike features represents a cutting-edge research challenge. Through crystal structure prediction and first-principles calculations, we systematically explore the Mg-P-H ternary system at 200 GPa, identifying two stable phases: kagome-structured MgP2H3 a…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094525] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Superconductivity in the kagomelike ${\mathrm{MgP}}_{2}{\mathrm{H}}_{3}$ under high pressure
item.dc:creator: Jianning Zhang, Xiuxing Zhang, Bing He, and Lianqiang Xu

item.title: Buckling and flat bands in twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Jannes van Poppelen and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) with its flat bands provides a rich platform for exploring emergent electronic orders. Similarly, periodically buckled monolayer graphene (MLG) has been proposed as a tunable alternative for realizing flat bands. Here, we investigate the combined effect of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115154] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jannes van Poppelen and Annica M. Black-Schaffer

Magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene (TBG) with its flat bands provides a rich platform for exploring emergent electronic orders. Similarly, periodically buckled monolayer graphene (MLG) has been proposed as a tunable alternative for realizing flat bands. Here, we investigate the combined effect of …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115154] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026

item.title: Origin of metallic state in NiO under high pressure: A first-principles investigation
item.description:
Author(s): Bei-Lei Liu, Yue-Chao Wang, Yuan-Ji Xu, Xingyu Gao, Hai-Feng Liu, and Hai-Feng Song

At ambient conditions, NiO is a prototypical antiferromagnetic correlated insulator. According to conventional Mott physics, applied pressure is expected to reduce the band gap and induce an insulator-to-metal transition. However, first-principles simulations consistently predict metallization press…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125145] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bei-Lei Liu, Yue-Chao Wang, Yuan-Ji Xu, Xingyu Gao, Hai-Feng Liu, and Hai-Feng Song

At ambient conditions, NiO is a prototypical antiferromagnetic correlated insulator. According to conventional Mott physics, applied pressure is expected to reduce the band gap and induce an insulator-to-metal transition. However, first-principles simulations consistently predict metallization press…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125145] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Origin of metallic state in NiO under high pressure: A first-principles investigation

item.title: Role of stacking and strain in mean-field magnetic moments of multilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): András Balogh, János Koltai, Péter Nemes-Incze, and Zoltán Tajkov

Rhombohedral or ABC-stacked multilayer graphene hosts a correlated magnetic ground state at charge neutrality, making it one of the simplest systems to investigate strong electronic correlations. We investigate this ground state in multilayer graphene structures using the Hubbard model in a distance…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125147] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): András Balogh, János Koltai, Péter Nemes-Incze, and Zoltán Tajkov

Rhombohedral or ABC-stacked multilayer graphene hosts a correlated magnetic ground state at charge neutrality, making it one of the simplest systems to investigate strong electronic correlations. We investigate this ground state in multilayer graphene structures using the Hubbard model in a distance…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125147] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Role of stacking and strain in mean-field magnetic moments of multilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: András Balogh, János Koltai, Péter Nemes-Incze, and Zoltán Tajkov

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral-spin modes
item.description:
Author(s): Mojdeh Saleh, Abhishek Kumar, Dmitrii L. Maslov, and Saurabh Maiti

The Rashba effect is the cornerstone of fascinating quantum phenomena like spin-charge conversion via the Edelstein effect and its inverse, the spin-Hall effect and its inverse, etc. These have proved to be the foundation for spintronics. However, much of these effects focused on the diffusive regime of electron motion. In spin-orbit coupled systems of the Rashba type, there is a special type of collective spin excitations, known as chiral-spin modes (CSMs). Here, the authors demonstrate that if such systems are probed close to the frequency of the CSMs, the spin-charge conversion effects exhibit a resonant enhancement. Using the polarization properties of the CSMs, the authors also suggest an architecture for spin pumping using THz pulses without the use of magnetic materials that offers control over the polarization of the injected spins. This work offers an avenue to push spintronics to the THz regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099602] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mojdeh Saleh, Abhishek Kumar, Dmitrii L. Maslov, and Saurabh Maiti

The Rashba effect is the cornerstone of fascinating quantum phenomena like spin-charge conversion via the Edelstein effect and its inverse, the spin-Hall effect and its inverse, etc. These have proved to be the foundation for spintronics. However, much of these effects focused on the diffusive regime of electron motion. In spin-orbit coupled systems of the Rashba type, there is a special type of collective spin excitations, known as chiral-spin modes (CSMs). Here, the authors demonstrate that if such systems are probed close to the frequency of the CSMs, the spin-charge conversion effects exhibit a resonant enhancement. Using the polarization properties of the CSMs, the authors also suggest an architecture for spin pumping using THz pulses without the use of magnetic materials that offers control over the polarization of the injected spins. This work offers an avenue to push spintronics to the THz regime.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 099602] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral-spin modes
item.dc:creator: Mojdeh Saleh, Abhishek Kumar, Dmitrii L. Maslov, and Saurabh Maiti
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Technical Perspective

item.title: Coexistence of long-range magnetic order and dynamical magnetism in the V-based kagome metals: A combined thermodynamic and $μ\mathrm{SR}$ study
item.description:
Author(s): Sheetal Devi, Yishui Zhou, Thomas J. Hicken, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Min-Kai Lee, Lieh-Jeng Chang, and Yixi Su

V-based kagome metals exhibit a unique lattice geometry that can give rise to exotic electronic and magnetic phenomena, making them an ideal platform to study the interplay of topology and magnetism. We present a combined thermodynamic and muon spin relaxation $(μ\mathrm{SR})$ investigation of singl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104443] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sheetal Devi, Yishui Zhou, Thomas J. Hicken, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Min-Kai Lee, Lieh-Jeng Chang, and Yixi Su

V-based kagome metals exhibit a unique lattice geometry that can give rise to exotic electronic and magnetic phenomena, making them an ideal platform to study the interplay of topology and magnetism. We present a combined thermodynamic and muon spin relaxation (μSR) investigation of single-crystal R…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104443] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Coexistence of long-range magnetic order and dynamical magnetism in the V-based kagome metals: A combined thermodynamic and $μ\mathrm{SR}$ study
item.dc:creator: Sheetal Devi, Yishui Zhou, Thomas J. Hicken, Zurab Guguchia, Hubertus Luetkens, Min-Kai Lee, Lieh-Jeng Chang, and Yixi Su

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Near-flat-band-driven violation of the Pauli limit in heavy fermion superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Yan-Xiao Wang and Yin Zhong

Heavy fermion superconductors often display upper critical fields that exceed the conventional Pauli paramagnetic limit, indicating that strong correlations and hybridized quasiparticle bands play an essential role in the paramagnetic pair-breaking process. Within the two-dimensional Kondo-Heisenber…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115152] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yan-Xiao Wang and Yin Zhong

Heavy fermion superconductors often display upper critical fields that exceed the conventional Pauli paramagnetic limit, indicating that strong correlations and hybridized quasiparticle bands play an essential role in the paramagnetic pair-breaking process. Within the two-dimensional Kondo-Heisenber…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115152] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Near-flat-band-driven violation of the Pauli limit in heavy fermion superconductors

item.title: Nature of magnetic exchange interactions in the kagome antiferromagnets FeGe and FeSn
item.description:
Author(s): Yitao Zheng, Yan Zhu, and Jun Hu

Magnetic exchange interactions (MEIs) in kagome magnets exhibit rich features due to the interplay of charge, spin, orbital and lattice degrees of freedom, giving rise to a variety of exotic quantum states. Through first-principles calculations, we systematically investigate the MEIs in kagome antif…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094445] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yitao Zheng, Yan Zhu, and Jun Hu

Magnetic exchange interactions (MEIs) in kagome magnets exhibit rich features due to the interplay of charge, spin, orbital and lattice degrees of freedom, giving rise to a variety of exotic quantum states. Through first-principles calculations, we systematically investigate the MEIs in kagome antif…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094445] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Nature of magnetic exchange interactions in the kagome antiferromagnets FeGe and FeSn

item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: Anisotropic fully gapped superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$
item.description:
Author(s): M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

Superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$ emerges from an exotic, nonmetallic normal state that exhibits signatures of Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid behavior, emergent symmetry, and excitonic order. The high upper critical field, ${H}_{c2}$, in ${…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094524] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

Superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional Li0.9Mo6O17 emerges from an exotic, nonmetallic normal state that exhibits signatures of Tomonaga-Luttinger liquid behavior, emergent symmetry, and excitonic order. The high upper critical field, Hc2, in Li0.9Mo6O17 suggests that that the favored pairing st…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 094524] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic fully gapped superconductivity in quasi-one-dimensional ${\mathrm{Li}}_{0.9}{\mathrm{Mo}}_{6}{\mathrm{O}}_{17}$
item.dc:creator: M. J. Grant, T. M. Huijbregts, R. Nicholls, M. Greenblatt, P. Chudzinski, A. Carrington, and N. E. Hussey

item.title: Magnetocrystalline anisotropy and three-dimensional hopping conduction at the surface of ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jarryd A. Horn, Yun Suk Eo, Keenan Avers, Hyeok Yoon, Ryan G. Dorman, Shanta R. Saha, and Johnpierre Paglione

Motivated by the recent discovery of metallic surface states in the $d$-electron Kondo insulator candidates FeSi and ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$, along with some recent reports of magnetic correlations in the surface transport properties of FeSi, we have investigated the low-temperature surface magnetotra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125142] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jarryd A. Horn, Yun Suk Eo, Keenan Avers, Hyeok Yoon, Ryan G. Dorman, Shanta R. Saha, and Johnpierre Paglione

Motivated by the recent discovery of metallic surface states in the d-electron Kondo insulator candidates FeSi and FeSb2, along with some recent reports of magnetic correlations in the surface transport properties of FeSi, we have investigated the low-temperature surface magnetotransport properties …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125142] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetocrystalline anisotropy and three-dimensional hopping conduction at the surface of ${\mathrm{FeSb}}_{2}$

item.title: Bandwidth-enhanced moiré superchiral optical field in a nodal ring photonic crystal
item.description:
Author(s): Qiang Zhang, Ruiwen Chen, and Xiaocong Yuan

On-chip optical superchiral fields are usually generated using nanostructures. Here we demonstrate superchiral field generation using surface waves in a one-dimensional photonic crystal (PhC). The PhC supports nodal rings featuring a bandwidth-enhanced, all-angle degenerate dispersion for transverse…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125429] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Qiang Zhang, Ruiwen Chen, and Xiaocong Yuan

On-chip optical superchiral fields are usually generated using nanostructures. Here we demonstrate superchiral field generation using surface waves in a one-dimensional photonic crystal (PhC). The PhC supports nodal rings featuring a bandwidth-enhanced, all-angle degenerate dispersion for transverse…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 125429] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Bandwidth-enhanced moiré superchiral optical field in a nodal ring photonic crystal

item.title: Effects of next-nearest neighbor hopping on the pairing and critical temperatures of the attractive Hubbard model on a square lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Fontenele, Natanael C. Costa, Thereza Paiva, and Raimundo R. dos Santos

The attractive Hubbard model plays a paradigmatic role in the study of superconductivity (superfluidity) and has become directly realizable in ultracold atom experiments on optical lattices. However, the critical temperatures, ${T}_{c}$'s, remain lower than the lowest temperatures currently achievab…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104519] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Fontenele, Natanael C. Costa, Thereza Paiva, and Raimundo R. dos Santos

The attractive Hubbard model plays a paradigmatic role in the study of superconductivity (superfluidity) and has become directly realizable in ultracold atom experiments on optical lattices. However, the critical temperatures, Tc's, remain lower than the lowest temperatures currently achievable in e…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 104519] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Effects of next-nearest neighbor hopping on the pairing and critical temperatures of the attractive Hubbard model on a square lattice
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo A. Fontenele, Natanael C. Costa, Thereza Paiva, and Raimundo R. dos Santos

item.title: Higher-dimensional overscreened fixed points and their deformations
item.description:
Author(s): Aleksandar Ljepoja, L. C. R. Wijewardhana, and Yashar Komijani

Non-Fermi liquids in dimensions $d>2$ remain poorly understood, particularly when relevant perturbations destabilize them. A prominent example is the overscreened Kondo fixed points in impurity models, and their one-dimensional analogs known as chirally stabilized fixed points. While such models …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115146] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Aleksandar Ljepoja, L. C. R. Wijewardhana, and Yashar Komijani

Non-Fermi liquids in dimensions d>2 remain poorly understood, particularly when relevant perturbations destabilize them. A prominent example is the overscreened Kondo fixed points in impurity models, and their one-dimensional analogs known as chirally stabilized fixed points. While such models ca…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115146] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Higher-dimensional overscreened fixed points and their deformations

item.title: Charge carrier relaxation dynamics in the one-dimensional Kondo lattice model
item.description:
Author(s): Arturo Perez-Romero, Mica Schwarm, and Fabian Heidrich-Meisner

A generic question in the field of ultrafast dynamics is concerned with the relaxation dynamics and the subsequent thermalization of optically excited charge carriers. Among several possible relaxation channels available in a solid-state system, we focus on the coupling to magnetic excitations. In t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115147] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arturo Perez-Romero, Mica Schwarm, and Fabian Heidrich-Meisner

A generic question in the field of ultrafast dynamics is concerned with the relaxation dynamics and the subsequent thermalization of optically excited charge carriers. Among several possible relaxation channels available in a solid-state system, we focus on the coupling to magnetic excitations. In t…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 115147] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
